items.title: May 27, 1985: Boston's Scott Wedman scores 26 PTS (11-11 FG/4-4 3P) off the bench in the Celtics' 148-114 win over the Lakers in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 27, 1985: Boston's Scott Wedman scores 26 PTS (11-11 FG/4-4 3P) off the bench in the Celtics' 148-114 win over the Lakers in Game 1 of the NBA Finals.

Kevin McHale also had 26 PTS for the Celtics, who led 79-49 at halftime. James Worthy scored 20 PTS to lead Los Angeles.

items.title: May 27, 1999: Portland’s Rasheed Wallace backs in and dunks against Utah during Game 6 of the WCSF.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 27, 1999: Portland’s Rasheed Wallace backs in and dunks against Utah during Game 6 of the WCSF.

Wallace finished with 14 PTS/3 STL and Isaiah Rider scored a game-high 24 PTS in a 92-80 victory that won the series for the Blazers. Jeff Hornacek scored 21 PTS to lead the Jazz.

items.title: Voting outside the consensus may not cost you your NBA awards ballot, per our research.
items.author: HoopsHype
items.body_excerpt:
Voting outside the consensus may not cost you your NBA awards ballot, per our research.

Across 12 NBA award seasons, the 10 biggest outliers each year were brought back as voters the next year 65 percent of the time. The 10 most consensus voters: 72.5 percent.

Not a brutal gap.
